message:
fix(trace): replace shallow event hashing with full payload commitment

The trace certificate verifier previously hashed only event kind and
sequence number, which meant two events with the same kind+seq but
different payloads (timestamps, region IDs, task IDs, obligation
details, cancel reasons) would produce identical hashes. A tampered
trace could swap payload contents between events of the same kind
without detection.

Now hash_trace_event() commits to the full event semantic structure:
- Version byte for forward-compatible hash evolution
- Logical time (committed as bytes, not Display)
- Complete payload fields via dedicated hash_* helpers for each
  event variant (spawn, complete, cancel, obligation, region, etc.)

Adds tests for:
- Payload sensitivity (different payloads → different hashes)
- Verifier rejection on mismatched payloads
- Backward compatibility with existing hash chains

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
